Explanation: Define the NLParseResponse interface — the contract between the edge function and the frontend. Covers initial_plan, modification, explanation, and comparison response types. Also defines ChatMessage types for the UI.

Action: Create the nl-parse edge function shell
Explanation: A Deno edge function that accepts POST { message, conversationHistory, currentPlan }, calls Claude Opus (claude-opus-4-6) via the Anthropic SDK, returns structured JSON. Follows the same pattern as existing create-checkout and stripe-webhook functions. Uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var. We use Opus for maximum extraction quality — will optimise model choice later.

The most critical single file. Tells Claude how to extract structured data from plain English. Includes: property type templates (inlined from property-defaults.json), Australian financial conventions, growth curve tiers, default assumptions (IO loans / 6.5% / 88% LVR), the JSON output schema, and 5+ diverse input/output examples. Must handle: ambiguity requiring decisions not clarification ('a few' → 4), numbers without units ('earning 240' → context decides $240k each or combined), Australian conventions (savings = monthly, income = annual).

Action: Deploy and test the edge function
Explanation: Deploy nl-parse to Supabase, set the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret, and test with curl. Verify it returns valid NLParseResponse JSON for a sample input like 'Jane and John both earning 120k with 80k deposit want to buy around 650k in VIC'.

Test the system prompt against 10 structured scenarios BEFORE building any UI. Scenarios: (1) single income first-timer, (2) couple equal income, (3) couple unequal income, (4) vague language — 'a few properties around 600k', (5) numbers without units — 'earning 240 saving 3500', (6) ambiguous price vs deposit — 'have about 650', (7) existing portfolio, (8) high income multi-property, (9) minimal info — 'a client wants to invest in QLD', (10) mixed units — '120k each and 3500 a month'. Fix prompt for any failures. This is the start of the iterative prompt loop — it continues throughout all phases.

Takes NLParseResponse.clientProfile and .investmentProfile → returns a partial InvestmentProfileData shape that can be passed to updateProfile(). Maps: deposit → depositPool, income → borrowingCapacity calc, savings → annualSavings, timeline → timelineYears, goals.

Takes NLParseResponse.properties[] → creates PropertySelection (type counts), propertyOrder (sequence), and PropertyInstanceDetails for each property. Uses getPropertyInstanceDefaults() as the base template, then overlays Claude's values (price, state, growth tier, LVR, loan type). The other ~30 fields per property come from defaults.

Takes a modification-type NLParseResponse + current plan state → returns the specific context updates to apply. Handles: move property timing, change price, change state, add/remove property, change savings, change LVR. Each maps to specific context setter calls.

Action: Unit test the data mapper with hardcoded responses
Explanation: Before wiring to the edge function — feed hardcoded NLParseResponse objects through the mapper functions and verify the output matches what the contexts expect. This catches type mismatches early without needing the full pipeline.

Renders a single chat message. Four variants: user (dark bubble / right-aligned), assistant (light bubble / left-aligned with small bot avatar), system (centered green pill), and option-card (clickable list items with icon + bold title + one-line description + arrow). Styled with Tailwind + shadcn to match existing dashboard.

A compact clickable list item shown when the engine pushes back on a modification. Shows: coloured icon, bold title, one-line description, right arrow. On click fires an action payload that applies the suggested fix. Appears inline in the chat flow. e.g. 'Lower purchase price — Drop to $380k, affordable by mid-2026 →'

A bordered key-value card shown after initial extraction. Displays: Clients, Income, Savings, Available Deposit, Property 1 details, Ownership. Lets the BA confirm at a glance what was understood. This is the 'visual receipt' — not a blocker, just confirmation.

A small animated card that flashes the critical numbers (income + savings) as the plan starts generating. Appears briefly before the full summary card. Not a blocker — just a quick visual receipt of the numbers that matter most. If these are wrong everything is wrong so the BA catches it immediately.

Manages chat state: message history, loading state, conversation context. Handles: sending messages to the nl-parse edge function, receiving responses, maintaining conversation history for multi-turn context, error states. Calls the Supabase edge function via supabase.functions.invoke().

The main chat container — replaces InputDrawer as the primary left panel. Header shows 'PropPath AI / Portfolio Planning Assistant'. Scrollable message list + text input pinned to bottom with placeholder 'Describe a client scenario...'. Typing indicator (3 pulsing dots) while waiting. In-chat loading state: 'Building your optimal path now...' with spinner. Auto-scrolls to latest message. Accepts onPlanGenerated callback.

Hide the existing InputDrawer (Properties timeline + Client inputs panels) — do NOT delete the code or components. Comment out or conditionally render. ChatPanel becomes the primary left panel. The existing InputDrawer components (TimelinePanel, ClientInputsPanel) stay in the codebase untouched for potential reuse later.

When ChatPanel receives an initial_plan response: call mapToInvestmentProfile → InvestmentProfileContext.updateProfile(). Call mapToPropertySelections → PropertySelectionContext.setSelections() + PropertyInstanceContext.setInstances(). The existing useAffordabilityCalculator auto-recalculates. Charts auto-re-render.

Action: Add skeleton loading states to Dashboard
Explanation: When a plan is generating show skeleton placeholders on: summary cards (top), timeline chart (middle), detail cards (bottom). Skeleton appears within 200ms of sending a message. Uses Framer Motion for smooth skeleton → populated transitions.

Instead of waiting for full calc render in stages: micro-confirmation card flashes first (income + savings), then summary cards populate, then timeline chart draws, then bottom detail cards appear. Use state flags to stagger reveals with Framer Motion animations.

Full pipeline test. Type a scenario in chat → edge function returns JSON → data mapper converts → contexts update → engine calculates → charts render. Test with the same 10-scenario bank from step 1.5 but now through the full UI. Fix any prompt issues found. This is the second iteration of the prompt refinement loop.

Action: Extend edge function for intent classification
Explanation: When a plan already exists the edge function needs to classify: is this a modification / explanation / comparison? Add current plan state to the system prompt context so Claude can understand what 'property 2' or 'the first one' refers to.

When a modification comes in: map to context updates via mapModificationToUpdates → apply to contexts → engine recalculates → if feasibility passes update dashboard + confirm in chat. If feasibility FAILS → do NOT apply → pass failure to constraint feedback handler → show option cards.

Takes output from existing feasibilityChecker.ts (which properties fail and why) and existing suggestedFixes.ts (fix suggestions) → formats into ChatOptionCard objects. Translates engine-speak into conversational options. e.g. 'Only $62k available need $118k' → 3 option cards with specific fixes and real numbers.

When BA clicks an option card: apply that specific fix to the relevant context → engine recalculates → dashboard updates → confirm in chat ('Applied — dropped property 2 to $380k. Cashflow buffer now $2100/month.'). Each card carries an action payload with the exact context updates.

Action: Test modification flows + prompt refinement round 3
Explanation: Test: move a property earlier (should fail if unaffordable → show options), change a price, change state (stamp duty recalculates), add a property, remove a property, change savings rate, change LVR. Also test ambiguous modifications: 'make it cheaper' / 'push it back a bit' / 'what if we went higher LVR'. Fix prompt for any failures.

When BA asks 'why is cashflow negative in 2029': identify the relevant period from the question, pull chart data + property data at that period from useChartDataGenerator output, send data context to Claude via edge function, return plain-English explanation referencing real calculated numbers.

When the edge function classifies a message as type 'explanation': call explanationGenerator with the question + current chart data → render Claude's response in chat. The response must reference specific numbers from the actual engine output — never made-up figures.

Action: Test explanation flows + prompt refinement round 4
Explanation: Test: 'why is cashflow negative in 2029' / 'what happens to equity after property 3' / 'explain the dip' / 'how does the deposit work for property 2'. Verify explanations reference real numbers from the engine. Fix any prompt issues — this is the fourth refinement round.
Supabase migration: add a conversation_history JSONB column to the scenarios table. Stores the full chat message array alongside the scenario data.
When saving a scenario include the chat history. When loading a saved scenario restore the chat history into ChatPanel. BA picks up the conversation where they left off.

Action: Test client report and PDF export
Explanation: The existing client report button and PDF export read from the same contexts — so NL-generated plans should work already. Test with 2-3 NL-generated scenarios. Fix any edge cases where the report expects data the NL path doesn't set.

Action: Add error handling to edge function calls
Explanation: Handle: Claude API timeouts ('Thinking longer than usual...'), malformed responses (retry once then 'Something went wrong — try rephrasing'), rate limits, network failures. Never show raw errors. All error states render as friendly chat messages.

Track tokens used per user per month in Supabase. New table or column for token counts. Soft monthly limit (e.g. 500 plans/month). Show usage in settings page. Edge function logs token usage on each call.

Action: Final prompt refinement and edge case sweep
Explanation: Final round of prompt testing. Focus on the cases that broke in earlier rounds plus new edge cases: zero savings, very low deposit ($10k), very high income ($500k+), PPOR included, vague language ('a client wants to invest'), modification that makes entire plan infeasible. This is the polish round — targeting 97%+ accuracy.

'What about Brisbane instead of Melbourne for property 3?' → fork the scenario, run both through the engine, show side-by-side differences in chat. Uses existing MultiScenarioContext. Lower priority — only if time permits.
